dev.jeka.core.tool

Class KBean

  • java.lang.Object
    • dev.jeka.core.tool.KBean
  • Direct Known Subclasses:
    AppKBean, BaseKBean, DockerKBean, EclipseKBean, GitKBean, IntellijKBean, MavenKBean, NativeKBean, ProjectKBean, SetupKBean

    public abstract class KBean
extends java.lang.Object
    Base class for KBean. User code is not supposed to instantiate KBeans using 'new' but usinng JkRunbase.load(java.lang.Class).

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Modifier Constructor and Description
      protected KBean()
      Use init() instead!

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      KBean cleanOutput()
      Cleans output directory.
      <T extends KBean>
      java.util.Optional<T>      		 find(java.lang.Class<T> beanClass) 
      java.nio.file.Path getBaseDir()
      Returns the base directory of the project.
      java.lang.String getBaseDirName()
      Returns the name of the folder which stands for the project base directory.
      java.nio.file.Path getBasePath(java.lang.String relativePath)
      Resolves the given relative path against the base directory of the project.
      JkImportedKBeans getImportedKBeans()
      Refer to the KBeans coming from other sub-runbase, that has been imported in this KBean.
      java.nio.file.Path getOutputDir()
      Returns the output directory where all the final and intermediate artifacts are generated.
      JkRunbase getRunbase()
      Returns the JkRunbase where this KBean has been instantiated.
      protected void init()
      This method is called by JeKa engine, right after public fields from command-line or properties have been injected.
      <T extends KBean>
      T      		 load(java.lang.Class<T> beanClass) 
      java.lang.String toString() 

      • Methods inherited from class java.lang.Object

        clone, equals, finalize, getClass, hashCode, notify, notifyAll, wait, wait, wait

    • Constructor Detail

      • KBean

        protected KBean()
        Use init() instead!

        Code added here won't work as expected since public fields (such as those from command-line properties, basedir, and imported KBeans) are not yet initialized.

    • Method Detail

      • init

        protected void init()
        This method is called by JeKa engine, right after public fields from command-line or properties have been injected.

        Put your initialization/configuration code here.

      • getBaseDir

        public final java.nio.file.Path getBaseDir()
        Returns the base directory of the project. In single projects, base dir = working dir. When working in multi-project (aka multi-module project), the base dir will be the sub-project base directory.

      • getBasePath

        public final java.nio.file.Path getBasePath(java.lang.String relativePath)
        Resolves the given relative path against the base directory of the project.

      • getBaseDirName

        public final java.lang.String getBaseDirName()
        Returns the name of the folder which stands for the project base directory.

      • getOutputDir

        public final java.nio.file.Path getOutputDir()
        Returns the output directory where all the final and intermediate artifacts are generated.

      • getImportedKBeans

        public final JkImportedKBeans getImportedKBeans()
        Refer to the KBeans coming from other sub-runbase, that has been imported in this KBean.

      • getRunbase

        public final JkRunbase getRunbase()
        Returns the JkRunbase where this KBean has been instantiated.

      • load

        public final <T extends KBean> T load(java.lang.Class<T> beanClass)
        See Also:
        JkRunbase.load(Class)

      • find

        public final <T extends KBean> java.util.Optional<T> find(java.lang.Class<T> beanClass)
        See Also:
        JkRunbase.find(Class)

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object

      • cleanOutput

        public KBean cleanOutput()
        Cleans output directory.
